rationale.R1.2: A section review centrally includes checking whether the stated conclusions follow coherently from the reported ablation results.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact reports numerical outcomes and then makes claims about performance effects, so verifying claim-support alignment is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: A generic section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register checks.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer must disclose uncertainty because the section lacks full experimental context and external tables.
rationale.RX.2: The reviewer must guard against stronger causal or necessity claims than the reported results justify.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to reviewing the provided section, not executing edits or auditing the whole paper.
rationale.RX.4: The reviewer should avoid feedback that could promote misleading scientific reporting or unsupported benchmark claims.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should remain traceable to the provided section text and reported numbers.
